product.name: Tero.Ops
product.tagline: Enterprise Asset Management for maritime and industrial operations — from vessel to shore, online or offline

capabilities.label: Hierarchical asset management
capabilities.claim: Models fleets, vessels, equipment, and sub-components in parent-child hierarchies with full maintenance history, QR codes, and associated spare parts — the way maritime operators actually organize their assets

evidence_internal.value: Assets support unlimited nesting via parentId, with breadcrumb navigation and tree views

capabilities.label: Automated preventive maintenance scheduling
capabilities.claim: Schedules maintenance jobs on time-based intervals, meter-based thresholds (e.g., engine hours), or whichever-comes-first logic — automatically generating the next job occurrence when work is completed

evidence_internal.value: Job occurrence types: ONE_OFF, TIME_BASED, METER_BASED, WHICHEVER_HAPPENS_FIRST. Meter readings trigger jobs when threshold crossed.

capabilities.label: Offline-first mobile app
capabilities.claim: Full app functionality on iOS, Android, and web — works completely offline with encrypted on-device storage and automatic sync when connectivity returns, designed for maritime environments with intermittent connectivity

evidence_internal.value: Built with Capacitor 6.x for native iOS/Android. Offline data encrypted and scoped to user permissions.

capabilities.label: Complete procurement workflow
capabilities.claim: End-to-end procurement from inventory request to purchase order to partial receipt — tracks stock levels with min/max/reorder thresholds, supports partial deliveries across multiple ports, and automatically updates inventory quantities

evidence_internal.value: Order status flow: OPEN → SENT_TO_SUPPLIER → PARTIALLY_RECEIVED → RECEIVED. Stock status: In Stock / Low Stock / No Stock based on quantity vs thresholds.

capabilities.label: Project and work request management
capabilities.claim: Groups related work requests into projects for large initiatives like drydocks and equipment overhauls — tracks requests through 8 workflow states from unassigned to completed with contractor assignment and priority management

evidence_internal.value: Project statuses: PLANNING/ACTIVE/COMPLETED/CANCELLED. Request statuses: UNASSIGNED, ASSIGNED, NOT_STARTED, PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, DELAYED, COMPLETED, CANCELLED.

capabilities.label: Multi-worksite fleet operations
capabilities.claim: Manages multiple companies, fleets, and worksites (vessels, fish farms, processing plants, warehouses) from a single platform with context switching and role-based access scoped to each worksite

evidence_internal.value: Worksite types: VESSEL, FISH_FARM, PLANT, WAREHOUSE. Users have per-worksite role assignments.

capabilities.label: Meter and consumption tracking
capabilities.claim: Tracks resource consumption (fuel, engine hours, water, electricity) through meters linked to assets — manual readings or IoT sensor data trigger meter-based maintenance jobs when thresholds are crossed

evidence_internal.value: Meters have: name, unit, serial number, usage_per_day. Measures store individual readings as BigDecimal strings.

capabilities.label: QR code-based field workflows
capabilities.claim: Every entity (assets, orders, inventory items, jobs) gets a printable QR code that field technicians scan with the mobile app to instantly navigate to the correct record, create work requests, or record maintenance work

evidence_internal.value: QR format: ENTITY_TYPE/companyId/worksiteId/entityId. Available on all major entities.

capabilities.label: Regulatory compliance and audit support
capabilities.claim: Auto-generates ISM Code compliance reports, flags missing documents and expired certificates, maintains full audit trails for port state control and classification society audits — supports both Icelandic (Samgöngustofa) and IMO standard templates

evidence_internal.value: Aggregates vessel logs, inspection records, maintenance history, drill records, crew certifications for compliance reporting.

capabilities.label: Digital vessel inspection checklists
capabilities.claim: Structured inspection checklists on phones and tablets with offline support, photo attachment at item level with auto-timestamping and geolocation, automatic deficiency follow-up task creation, and inspection records ready for regulatory audits

evidence_internal.value: Inspection module v2.0 with responsibility matrix assignment for deficiency follow-up.

capabilities.label: Large-scale data import with progress tracking
capabilities.claim: Imports up to 50,000 records at once with real-time progress tracking, PostgreSQL COPY for maximum throughput, and downloadable error reports for failed rows — enables maritime companies to onboard entire fleet maintenance histories in minutes

evidence_internal.value: Processes in 1,000-record chunks. Reduces import time by 80% for large datasets.

capabilities.label: Bilingual platform (English and Icelandic)
capabilities.claim: Full platform and documentation support in both English and Icelandic, with native maritime terminology in both languages — not machine-translated but natively authored for each language

evidence_internal.value: i18n system with dedicated Icelandic glossary of maritime terms. Quality gate ensures Icelandic content uses proper native terminology.

capabilities.label: Passwordless magic link authentication
capabilities.claim: Frictionless passwordless authentication via magic links with JWT tokens, role-based permissions scoped to company and worksite level, and optional two-factor authentication via email, SMS, or TOTP

evidence_internal.value: JWT tokens stored in localStorage. Token refresh interceptor handles expiration. Supports EMAIL, SMS, TOTP 2FA.

differentiators.label: Purpose-built for maritime and fishing operations
differentiators.claim: Designed from the ground up for maritime operations — not a generic EAM adapted for vessels. Models fleets, vessels, fish farms, and processing plants with native maritime workflows like partial delivery across ports, meter-triggered maintenance for engine hours, and ISM Code compliance

evidence_internal.value: Worksite types include VESSEL, FISH_FARM, PLANT, WAREHOUSE. Release notes reference fishing vessels, spare parts at different ports, Samgöngustofa compliance.

differentiators.label: Offline-first design for sea operations
differentiators.claim: Unlike cloud-only EAM platforms, Tero.Ops works fully offline on vessels at sea with encrypted on-device storage and automatic sync — crews can record maintenance, inspections, and meter readings without internet connectivity

evidence_internal.value: Capacitor native apps with offline-first architecture. Data encrypted on device, scoped to user permissions.

differentiators.label: Native Icelandic language and maritime terminology
differentiators.claim: The only EAM platform with native Icelandic language support and proper Icelandic maritime terminology — not machine-translated but authored with domain-specific fishing and aquaculture vocabulary

evidence_internal.value: Dedicated Icelandic glossary with maritime terms. Quality gate rejects anglicisms and ensures native terminology.

differentiators.label: Bridges the sea-shore operational gap
differentiators.claim: Solves the 'sea/shore problem' — what happens on the vessel stays visible to shore-side management in real time when connected, and syncs automatically when vessels return to connectivity, maintaining a single source of truth across distributed operations

evidence_internal.value: Multi-worksite architecture with context switching. Fleet managers see all vessels. Crew sees only their vessel data.

use_cases.label: Fleet preventive maintenance
use_cases.problem: Cannot track maintenance schedules across multiple vessels — relies on paper logs and spreadsheets that are outdated by the time vessels return to port
use_cases.outcome: Sees real-time maintenance status across entire fleet, with automated job scheduling based on engine hours and calendar intervals, reducing unplanned downtime

use_cases.label: Spare parts and inventory management
use_cases.problem: Parts requests from vessels arrive by phone or email with no tracking — orders get lost, duplicated, or arrive at the wrong port
use_cases.outcome: Structured request-to-order-to-receipt workflow with partial delivery support, ensuring the right parts reach the right vessel at the right port

use_cases.label: ISM Code compliance and audit readiness
use_cases.problem: Preparing for port state control inspections and classification society audits requires manually gathering documents from multiple paper systems, risking gaps that lead to vessel detentions
use_cases.outcome: One-click compliance reports aggregating vessel logs, inspection records, maintenance history, drill records, and crew certifications — with gap analysis flagging missing or expired documents before audits

use_cases.label: Multi-port procurement for fishing vessels
use_cases.problem: Fishing vessels receive parts at different ports during trips — full-order approval blocks processing of received items while waiting for remaining deliveries
use_cases.outcome: Approves individual line items within purchase orders, processing partial deliveries independently without blocking the full order workflow

use_cases.label: Mobile maintenance recording at sea
use_cases.problem: Cannot record maintenance work while at sea because cloud-based systems require internet — paper logs get lost or arrive weeks late
use_cases.outcome: Records maintenance work, takes inspection photos, submits work requests, and reads meter values on phone or tablet — all works offline and syncs automatically when connectivity returns

use_cases.label: Drydock and overhaul project coordination
use_cases.problem: Drydock projects involve dozens of work items across multiple contractors with no centralized tracking — scope creep and missed items are common
use_cases.outcome: Groups all drydock work requests into a single project, tracks each through 8 workflow states, assigns contractors, and maintains full documentation with photos and comments

use_cases.label: Fleet asset value and risk visibility
use_cases.problem: Has no consolidated view of fleet maintenance status, compliance gaps, or procurement costs — decisions are based on incomplete information from various departments
use_cases.outcome: Dashboard showing fleet-wide maintenance status, compliance gaps, procurement spending, and operational metrics — enabling data-driven decisions about fleet investment and risk

displacement_points.label: Maritime-native vs. generic-adapted
displacement_points.claim: Unlike IBM Maximo, Fiix, or generic CMMS platforms that require heavy customization for maritime use, Tero.Ops ships with vessel-specific workflows, fleet hierarchy, port-based procurement, and ISM Code compliance out of the box

displacement_points.label: True offline vs. cloud-dependent
displacement_points.claim: AMOS, Danaos, and most maritime CMMS require connectivity or run on expensive onboard servers — Tero.Ops works fully offline on standard mobile devices with encrypted local storage and automatic sync

displacement_points.label: Modern mobile-first UX vs. legacy desktop
displacement_points.claim: Legacy maritime CMMS platforms like AMOS and BASS were designed for desktop use — Tero.Ops is mobile-first with QR scanning, photo capture, and touch-optimized interfaces designed for use on vessels

displacement_points.label: Structured digital records vs. paper/spreadsheet chaos
displacement_points.claim: Replaces spreadsheets and paper logs with structured, searchable, auditable digital records — maintenance history never gets lost, compliance documents are always current, and inventory levels update automatically

displacement_points.label: Native Icelandic language support
displacement_points.claim: The only maritime EAM with native Icelandic language support and proper maritime terminology — critical for Icelandic fishing fleet crews who work in Icelandic

constraints.label: Not a full ERP system
constraints.claim: Tero.Ops is an EAM/CMMS focused on asset management, maintenance, and procurement — it does not replace accounting, payroll, HR, or general business management systems

constraints.label: Not for consumer or personal use
constraints.claim: Designed for commercial fleet and industrial operations — not for personal boat owners, recreational vessel maintenance, or consumer vehicle tracking

constraints.label: Not a vessel navigation or tracking system
constraints.claim: Manages assets and maintenance, not vessel navigation, route planning, AIS tracking, or real-time vessel positioning — integrates with but does not replace VMS or AIS systems

constraints.label: Not a catch management or quota system
constraints.claim: Manages vessel equipment and maintenance, not fish catch reporting, quota allocation, or fisheries resource management
